content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 2, 1993

Makoto Takashina, AIA
425 Mission Avenue
San Rafael, CA 94901

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-227

Dear Mr. Takashina:

This is in response to your request for advice under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/
This letter will also serve to confirm telephone advice provided
to you on May 11, 1993.

QUESTION

As an architect and member of the Design Review Board of the
City of San Rafael, may you represent a client by presenting
drawings and plans to the design review board.

CONCLUSION

Provided you meet all of the criteria set forth in Regulation
18700.1, you may represent your client's drawings and plans before
the design review board, the planning commission and the city
staff.

FACTS

You are a sole practitioner doing business in the City of San
Rafael. In addition, you are a member of the city's design review
board (the "board").

The board is an advisory body to the planning commission. No
member of the planning commission serves as a member of the board.
All of the members of the board are comprised of architects or
landscape architects.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-93-227
Page Two


ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 provides that no public official shall make,
participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or
has reason to know he has a financial interest. An official has a
financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on any source of income
aggregating $250 or more provided to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section
87103(c).)

     A public official "makes a governmental decision" when he or
she votes on a matter, obligates the agency to a course of action,
or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of the agency.
(Regulation 18700(b).) An official "attempts to use his or her
official position to influence a decision" if the official
contracts, appears before, or otherwise attempts to influence any
member, officer, employee or consultant of the agency.
(Regulation 18700.1(a), copy enclosed.)

     Two exceptions to this rule apply. Commission Regulation
18700.1(b)(4) allows a public official to prepare drawings or
submissions of an architectural, engineering or similar nature to
be used by a client in connection with a proceeding before any
agency. However, this provision applies only if the official has
no other direct oral or written contact with the agency with
regard to the client's proceeding before the agency except for
necessary contact with agency staff concerning the processing or
evaluation of the drawings or submissions prepared by the
official.

     This section would only permit a public official to do such
things as answer inquiries from agency staff concerning a client's
plans or drawings.

     The second exception applies specifically to members of a
design review board.

     Commission Regulation 18700.1(b)(5) provides that an official
is not attempting to use his or her official position to influence
a decision when the official appears before a design or
architectural review committee or similar body of which he or she
is a member to present drawings or submissions of an
architectural, engineering or similar nature which the official
has prepared for a client if the following three criteria are met:

          (A) The review committee's sole function is to
     review architectural or engineering plans or designs and
     to make recommendations in that instance concerning
     those plans or designs to a planning commission or other
     agency;

File No. A-93-227
Page Three


     (B)  The ordinance or other provision of law
requires that the review committee include architects,
engineers or persons in related professions, and the
official was appointed to the body to fulfill this
requirement; and

     (C)  The official is a sole practitioner.

                         Regulation 18700.1(b)(5)

     You indicated in your letter that it is your understanding
that the board is an advisory body to the planning commission.
You also indicated that you are a sole practitioner.  It appears
that the first and third criteria under Regulation 18700.1(b)(5)
are met.  The second criteria requires that the ordinance or other
provision of law creating the board requires that the committee
include architects, engineers or persons in related professions.
If this is a requirement under the city's ordinance, then the
second criteria would also be met.  The information you provided
was not sufficient to make a definitive determination on this
point.

     Accordingly, provided that all three criteria of Regulation
18700.1(b)(5) apply, you would not be prohibited from appearing
before the design review board to present drawings or submissions
on behalf of a client.

     Please note however that Regulation 18700.1 prohibits you
from using your official position to influence the decision of
another agency.  If you appear before the planning commission
representing a client, you may not make such an appearance
purporting to represent the views and opinions as a member of the
design review board.

     I trust this confirms our earlier conversation sufficiently.
If you have any other questions, please feel free to contact the
Commission's Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Jeanette E. Turvill
                                            Political Reform Consultant
                                            Legal Division

JM/JET/jt
